name: Sputnik 1
description: The world's first artificial satellite, a 58 cm polished metal sphere that transmitted radio pulses from low Earth orbit.
achievements: First artificial Earth satellite
First human-made object in orbit
Triggered the Space Race

name: Explorer 1
description: The first successful U.S. satellite, launched on a Jupiter-C rocket, carrying a cosmic ray detector.
achievements: First U.S. satellite
Discovered the Van Allen radiation belts
First major U.S. scientific space discovery

name: Luna 2
description: The first spacecraft to reach the surface of the Moon, impacting near the Sea of Serenity.
achievements: First spacecraft to reach the Moon
First object from Earth to reach another celestial body

name: Vostok 1
description: The first human spaceflight, carrying cosmonaut Yuri Gagarin on a single orbit of Earth lasting 108 minutes.
achievements: First human in space
First human orbital flight
First safe return of a human from space

name: Friendship 7
description: The first U.S. orbital spaceflight, carrying John Glenn on three orbits of Earth aboard a Mercury capsule.
achievements: First American to orbit Earth
Three successful orbital passes
Demonstrated U.S. human orbital capability

name: Mariner 4
description: The first spacecraft to perform a successful flyby of Mars and return close-up images of another planet.
achievements: First close-up images of Mars
First successful planetary flyby
Revealed thin Martian atmosphere and cratered surface

name: Apollo 11
description: The first crewed Moon landing; Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin walked on the lunar surface while Michael Collins orbited overhead.
achievements: First humans on the Moon
First Moon surface experiments (ALSEP)
First Moon sample return (21.5 kg)
First Moon surface extravehicular activity

name: Apollo 13
description: A crewed lunar mission aborted after an oxygen tank exploded; the crew survived by using the lunar module as a lifeboat.
achievements: Successful crew rescue from deep space emergency
Demonstrated spacecraft systems redundancy
Advanced emergency space operations procedures

name: Pioneer 10
description: The first spacecraft to travel through the asteroid belt and make a close flyby of Jupiter, before heading into interstellar space.
achievements: First spacecraft through the asteroid belt
First close-up observations of Jupiter
First spacecraft to escape the solar system on a trajectory
Carried Pioneer plaque — first interstellar message

name: Viking 1
description: The first spacecraft to successfully land on Mars and return data from the surface, operating for over six years.
achievements: First successful Mars landing
First panoramic photos from Mars surface
First Mars soil biology experiments
Mars weather monitoring over multiple years

name: Voyager 1
description: Launched to study the outer solar system, Voyager 1 performed flybys of Jupiter and Saturn and is now the most distant human-made object in space, operating in interstellar space.
achievements: Detailed flybys of Jupiter and Saturn
Discovery of active volcanoes on Io
Detailed imaging of Saturn's rings
First spacecraft to enter interstellar space (2012)
Most distant human-made object ever

name: Voyager 2
description: The only spacecraft to have flown by all four giant outer planets — Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune — providing humanity's first close-up views of Uranus and Neptune.
achievements: Only spacecraft to visit Uranus and Neptune
Discovery of geysers on Neptune's moon Triton
Discovery of rings around Uranus
Second spacecraft to enter interstellar space (2018)

name: Hubble Space Telescope
description: A large optical/ultraviolet/near-infrared space telescope in low Earth orbit, providing unprecedentedly sharp images of the universe.
achievements: Determined precise age of the universe (~13.8 billion years)
Confirmed accelerating expansion of the universe
Deep field images revealing thousands of galaxies
Studied atmospheres of exoplanets
Over 1.5 million scientific observations

name: Mars Pathfinder
description: A lander and the first Mars rover (Sojourner) that demonstrated airbag landing technology and rover mobility on another planet.
achievements: First Mars rover (Sojourner)
Demonstrated airbag landing system
Analyzed Martian rocks with APXS spectrometer
First live internet coverage of a planetary mission

name: Cassini-Huygens
description: A joint NASA/ESA mission consisting of the Cassini Saturn orbiter and the Huygens probe that landed on Titan, Saturn's largest moon.
achievements: Orbited Saturn for 13 years
Huygens probe landed on Titan — first landing in outer solar system
Discovered liquid methane lakes on Titan
Discovered water vapor plumes from Enceladus
Detailed mapping of Saturn's rings and moons

name: International Space Station (ISS)
description: A modular space station in low Earth orbit representing a permanent human presence in space, built collaboratively by 15 nations.
achievements: Continuous human presence in space since November 2000
Largest human-made structure in space
Over 250 residents from 19 countries
Thousands of scientific experiments conducted
International cooperation in space operations

name: Mars Odyssey
description: A long-lived Mars orbiter that mapped the planet's surface minerals and detected water ice near the poles.
achievements: Detected large water ice deposits at Martian poles
Global thermal infrared mapping of Mars
Longest-operating spacecraft at Mars
Acted as communications relay for Mars surface missions

name: Mars Express
description: ESA's first planetary mission, orbiting Mars and providing radar subsurface sounding and high-resolution stereo imaging.
achievements: First radar detection of subsurface water on Mars
High-resolution stereo surface mapping
Detection of methane in Martian atmosphere
Beagle 2 lander (failed to fully deploy)

name: Spirit
description: One of the Mars Exploration Rover twins, Spirit operated in Gusev Crater for over six years, far beyond its planned 90-day mission.
achievements: Discovered evidence of ancient water activity in Martian rocks
Operated for 2,208 Martian days (sols)
Climbed the Columbia Hills range
Studied volcanic and hydrothermal rock formations

name: Opportunity
description: The Mars Exploration Rover twin of Spirit, Opportunity explored the Meridiani Planum region for nearly 15 years and traveled over 45 km.
achievements: Found hematite spheres (blueberries) indicating past liquid water
Longest distance driven on another world (45.16 km)
Operated for 5,111 Martian days
Explored Endeavour Crater rim
Discovered clay minerals indicating neutral pH ancient water

name: MESSENGER
description: The first spacecraft to orbit Mercury, mapping its surface completely and investigating its geology, magnetic field, and thin exosphere.
achievements: First Mercury orbiter
Complete global mapping of Mercury
Confirmed water ice in permanently shadowed polar craters
Discovered volcanic plains and unusual dark material
Characterized Mercury's magnetic field

name: New Horizons
description: The first spacecraft to perform a close flyby of Pluto, revealing a complex world with nitrogen glaciers and a tenuous atmosphere, before continuing into the Kuiper Belt.
achievements: First close-up images and data from Pluto and Charon
Discovered Tombaugh Regio nitrogen ice plain
Revealed haze layers in Pluto's atmosphere
Flyby of Kuiper Belt object Arrokoth (2019)

name: Mars Science Laboratory (Curiosity)
description: A large rover that landed in Gale Crater using the sky crane system, designed to assess whether Mars ever had conditions suitable for microbial life.
achievements: Demonstrated sky crane landing system
Confirmed ancient habitable environment in Gale Crater
Detected organic molecules in Martian rock
Measured radiation levels on Mars surface and during transit
Discovered complex geology of Mount Sharp (Aeolis Mons)

name: Juno
description: A solar-powered spacecraft orbiting Jupiter on a polar orbit to study its composition, gravity field, magnetic field, and polar magnetosphere.
achievements: Revealed chaotic cyclone clusters at Jupiter's poles
Precise measurement of Jupiter's gravity and interior structure
Discovered ammonia upwelling (Jovian mushballs)
First detailed images of Jupiter's polar regions
Extended mission to study Jovian moons

name: Parker Solar Probe
description: A spacecraft designed to orbit closer to the Sun than any previous mission, studying the solar corona and solar wind.
achievements: Closest approach to the Sun of any spacecraft
First spacecraft to fly through the solar corona (2021)
Measured electric fields driving solar wind acceleration
Observed dust-free zone near the Sun
Detected switchbacks in the solar wind

name: BepiColombo
description: A joint European-Japanese mission consisting of two orbiters to study Mercury's surface, interior, and magnetosphere in unprecedented detail.
achievements: First joint ESA-JAXA planetary mission
Multiple gravity assist flybys of Mercury during cruise phase
Scheduled to enter Mercury orbit in 2026

name: Chang'e 5
description: China's first lunar sample return mission, landing in the Oceanus Procellarum region and returning 1.731 kg of lunar material to Earth.
achievements: First lunar sample return since Soviet Luna 24 (1976)
First Chinese sample return from the Moon
Collected samples from a geologically young volcanic region
Confirmed volcanic activity on Moon as recently as ~2 billion years ago

name: DART (Double Asteroid Redirection Test)
description: The world's first planetary defense test mission, which deliberately impacted the asteroid Dimorphos to alter its orbital period around Didymos.
achievements: First successful kinetic impactor planetary defense demonstration
Shortened Dimorphos orbital period by 33 minutes
Proved asteroid deflection is feasible
Observed by LICIACube cubesat deployed before impact

name: James Webb Space Telescope
description: The most powerful space telescope ever built, observing in infrared wavelengths from the Sun-Earth L2 point to study the earliest galaxies, stellar formation, and exoplanet atmospheres.
achievements: Deepest infrared images of the universe
Detection of galaxies from within 400 million years of the Big Bang
First direct images of exoplanets with spectral data
Detailed atmospheric characterization of exoplanets
High-resolution imaging of solar system objects

name: Artemis I
description: An uncrewed test flight of the Space Launch System (SLS) and Orion spacecraft on a lunar free-return trajectory, validating systems for future crewed Moon missions.
achievements: First flight of NASA's Space Launch System (SLS)
First flight of the Orion spacecraft beyond low Earth orbit
Flew within 130 km of the lunar surface
Traveled 1.3 million km from Earth — farthest distance for a crewed-capable spacecraft
All primary mission objectives achieved

name: Chandrayaan-3
description: India's third lunar mission and first successful Moon landing, placing the Vikram lander and Pragyan rover near the lunar south pole.
achievements: India's first successful Moon landing
First landing near the lunar south pole
Detected sulfur and other elements in lunar south polar soil
Pragyan rover traversed approximately 100 meters on the surface
Made India the fourth country to land on the Moon

name: OSIRIS-REx
description: A mission to study and return a sample from near-Earth carbonaceous asteroid Bennu, returning 121.6 grams of pristine asteroid material to Earth in 2023.
achievements: Largest asteroid sample returned to Earth by NASA
Mapped and characterized Bennu's surface in detail
TAG (Touch-And-Go) sample collection from asteroid surface
Discovered Bennu is a rubble pile with active particle ejection
Spacecraft continued as OSIRIS-APEX mission to asteroid Apophis

name: InSight
description: A geophysical lander that studied the interior structure of Mars using a seismometer and heat flow probe, operating on the Martian surface for four years.
achievements: First detection of marsquakes
Characterized Mars interior layers using seismology
Measured heat flow from Martian interior
Recorded meteorite impacts on Mars in real-time
Detected magnetic field pulses at the landing site

name: Mars 2020 (Perseverance)
description: An advanced rover exploring Jezero Crater, collecting rock cores for eventual return to Earth and carrying the Ingenuity helicopter — the first powered aircraft to fly on another planet.
achievements: First powered flight on another planet (Ingenuity helicopter)
Collected over 20 rock core samples for future Earth return
Produced oxygen from Martian CO₂ (MOXIE experiment)
Recorded sounds of Mars
Characterized ancient lake delta environment in Jezero Crater
